name: Deadlock Avoidance (Banker's Algorithm)
description: The Banker's algorithm grants resource requests only if the system stays in a safe state: a request is tentatively allocated, then the safety algorithm seeks an ordering in which every process can finish; if such a sequence exists the request is granted, otherwise the tentative allocation is rolled back and denied.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Request resources"]
    N2{"Resources available?"}
    N3["Block / wait"]
    N4["Tentatively allocate"]
    N5["Run safety algorithm"]
    N6["Find finishable proc"]
    N7["Release its resources"]
    N8{"All can finish?"}
    N9{"Safe state?"}
    N10["Grant request"]
    N11["Roll back (deny)"]
    N12["Deadlock-free alloc"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 -->|no| N3
    N3 -->|wait| N2
    N2 -->|yes| N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N6
    N8 -->|yes| N9
    N9 -->|safe| N10
    N9 -->|unsafe| N11
    N10 --> N12
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Operating System Concepts (10th ed.)
sources.authors: Silberschatz, A.; Galvin, P. B.; Gagne, G.
